Description: <.1% Christian Adherents and no confirmed sustained movement.

A 5 tier red to green scale ranging from Unreached - Minimally Reached, Superficially Reached, Partially Reached, and Significantly Reached. This is based on the % evangelicals and % total Christian Adherents.

Global Status of Evangelical Christianity.

[scale runs 0-6] Higher = more reached

The Global Status of Evangelical Christianity (GSEC) is a model that describes the progress of the gospel among the peoples of the world by considering:

1. The extent to which a people group is Evangelical.
2. Accessibility to the gospel.
3. Church-planting activity, whether localized or widespread, within the past two years.

 The model establishes the criteria for identifying those people groups described as "Unreached People Groups".
<5% Christian Adherents & <2% Evangelical

Does the people group in question have more than 10% of the population adhering to a non-evangelical form of Christian faith? Exclusions: do not count Christian cults. List estimated percent, eg: “7.5%”
All Christian Adherents including nominal, historical, and most Christian cults, as well as evangelical.
All Evangelical Christian Adherents in a people group.

An 8 point scale (0-7) that estimates relative progress in engagement.
0) No prior known engagement.  
1) Restart Needed
2) Gospel Proclamation →Churches
3) Believers Disciples Baptism
4) Indigenous Local Church
5) Churches 2 Gen
6) Churches Multiplying 4 Gen
7) Sustained Gospel presence 10%+
A binary assessment if the people group is “engaged” or “unengaged.”

Connects to the column SPI.
0=Unengaged and Unreached.
1=Engaged yet Unreached
2=No Longer Unreached

A single digit, three-number scale to show how the IMB categorizes them in terms of strategic importance.
[scale: 0, 1, 2] 
0=Unengaged and Unreached.
1=Engaged yet Unreached
2=No Longer Unreached

A 4 point scale which measures # gospel workers meeting definition of engaged by population.
1) Unengaged
2) Barely Engaged
3) Moderately Engaged
4) Sufficiently Engaged.
Estimated # of gospel workers involved in evanglism, discipleship, & church planting.

Has this PG ever been engaged with the gospel previously at any time as far as we know? Scale: 
1. No known engagement ever.
2. Confirmed engagement in the past.
3. Potential past engagement, but no records exist.

Christianized people groups who were later islamicized mark with “x” after number. Example: 1X, or 2X, or 3X.
A 'yes' value for those PGs with no known engagement anywhere. Null values blank. Is this same People Group engaged with the gospel anywhere?
Is this engagement known to be a movement oriented engagement? Yes / No
Last time the engagement section was updated.

Levels of Verification: 
Have reports of engagement among this PG been independently verified by someone outside of the ministry responsible for the engagement?
V1 Observed / Reported by someone within network.
V2 Mapped, church or progress can be visualized by a gen map or equivalent.
V3 Internal Audit - someone from within the network but outside the reported stream has physically verified progress.
V4 External Audit. Someone outside the network has physically verified the progress.

The angular distance north or south of the earth's equator, measured in degrees along a meridian, as on a map or globe.

The arc or portion of the equator intersected between the meridian of a given place and the meridian of some other place from which longitude is reckoned, as from Greenwich, England, or sometimes from the capital of a country, as from Washington or Paris. 3 letter Language Code from HIS / Ethnologue
3 letter Language Code [5014 used in IMB]
Each three-letter code uniquely identifies one of the more than 7,000 living languages documented in the Ethnologue. 
Definition is based on a criterion of shared literature. If varieties of speech are similar enough to use the same literature, they are considered the same language; if they would need different literatures, they are considered different languages.

User Interface: People Group: AX’s unique PG ID# (PGAC)
Description:
AX Numbering System

Example: 01-JP-045983 = a PGAC number

Component Parts: 
AX (numeric) = Taken from the last two digits of the ROP1 code and used to broadly ID a PG.

SS (alpha)  = Source of PGAC #: Joshua Project (JP), IMB (IM), Etnopedia (ET) Accelerate Local (AX).

PGAC (numeric) = 
·       Joshua Project: The 5-digit PGID3 code used by Joshua Project. (Add a zero to make 6-digits)
·       IMB: 6-digit ROP25 
·       Etnopedia: 6-digit ROP25
·       AX: 6-digit sequential #  “XX1234”

User Interface: People Group: AX’s unique PG ID# (PGIC)
Description:
AX Numbering System

Example: 01-JP-045983-USA = A PGIC number

Component Parts: 
AX (numeric) = Taken from the last two digits of the ROP1 code and used to broadly ID a PG.

SS (alpha)  = Source of PGAC #: Joshua Project (JP), IMB (IM), Etnopedia (ET) Accelerate Local (AX).

PGAC (numeric) = 
·       Joshua Project: The 5-digit PGID3 code used by Joshua Project. (Add a zero to make 6-digits)
·       IMB: 6-digit ROP25 
·       Etnopedia: 6-digit ROP25
·       AX: 6-digit sequential #  “XX1234”

ISO3 (alpha) = “ABC” is PGIC indicator a 3-character alpha digit country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 standard)

ROP3 codes are the primary codes within ROP. ROP3 Codes are 6-digit, numeric fields that provide a unique identifier for each people. Each code is perpetual; it will not be used more than once, even if the people it identifies is removed from the database.
